subsection_fields.label: Personal Property Included
subsection_fields.narrative: There is no personal property (FF&E) included in this valuation. There is not any business or intangible value included in the value conclusion reported here.

fields.label: Location Description
fields.value: North Battleford, Saskatchewan, in a centrally situated residential area near key commercial corridors and the downtown core.

fields.label: Property Access & Frontage
fields.value: The property fronts 109 Street with convenient connections to major arterials including 100th Street/Highway 4 (north-south route to downtown and Highway 16) and Territorial Drive (east-west loop connecting retail and services).

fields.label: Public Transportation
fields.value: Local bus service operates on nearby corridors such as 100th Street and Territorial Drive, providing direct access to downtown, retail centres, and community facilities.

fields.label: Walk/Bike/Transit Scores
fields.value: Walk Score around 60, Transit Score near 35, and Bike Score around 55, reflecting car-optional access for daily needs.

fields.label: Local Area Description
fields.value: Mature urban district with a mix of single-family homes, small multi-unit buildings, and local businesses. Residents are close to grocery, cafes, and parks, with recreation and services clustered along 100th Street and Territorial Drive.

fields.label: Nearby Schools
value: North Battleford Comprehensive High School (Grades 10-12, public)

fields.value: Consists of one parcel with total site area based on information from ICS.

fields.label: Site Accessibility Summary
fields.value: Access to the subject site is considered average overall.

fields.label: Frontage & Site Access Description
fields.value: Property fronts on 109 Street with access to major arterials

fields.value: 1101 - 8 Furnaces, 1121 - Boilers with baseboard radiant heat
Wood interior & metal exterior doors/Vinyl or metal frame double pane glazing
Combination of carpet, tile, vinyl tile and laminate hard wood
Landscaping around the building perimeter to consist of shrubs and trees. The landscaping as proposed is well established and well maintained.

The subject provides 18 parking spaces and is therefore conforming to zoning requirements. The parking ratio of 1.1 per unit is within the typical range of 0.75-1.25 spaces per unit within zoning requirements.
12.9% (3,138 SF footprint / 24,400 SF site), which is within market standards (20-35%) for similar walkup buildings in the area.
The building features a functional 'Walkup design with typical site coverage and adequate off-street parking.
A Phase I report was not provided. This appraisal assumes that the improvements are constructed free of all hazardous waste and toxic substances.
materials, including (but not limited to) unseen asbestos and mold.

fields.value: Canada's economy in 2025 finds itself in a delicate balance...
Saskatchewan's economy in 2025 is defined by steady growth...
Saskatchewan's rental markets entered 2025 with improving balance...
The highest and best use of a property is defined as...
In this section the highest and best use of the subject as though vacant...
An evaluation of what is physically possible...
Based on the analysis of the subject's market...
There is only one use that creates value...
The legal factors influencing the highest and best use...
Five possible alternative treatments: redevelopment/demolition, expansion, renovation, conversion, continued use as-is
Based on the type of property and the income generating potential of the improvements
In traditional valuation theory, the three approaches to estimating the value of an asset are the cost approach, sales comparison approach, and income capitalization approach...
Characteristics specific to the subject property do not warrant that a site value is developed.
The cost approach considers the cost to replace the proposed improvements, less accrued depreciation, plus the market value of the land...
Cost Approach has not been undertaken
Investors typically do not place emphasis on replacement cost in establishing value for investment properties.
The sales comparison approach estimates value based on what other purchasers and sellers in the market have agreed to as price for comparable properties...
We have undertaken the Direct Comparison Approach as part of this assignment.

The income capitalization approach ("income approach") simulates the reasoning of an investor who views the cash flows that would result from the anticipated revenue and expense of a property throughout its lifetime...

As Stabilized (Fee Simple Estate) current market value for the property located at 1101, 1121 109 St, North Battleford, SK.

Valta Property Valuations Ltd. is proud to present the appraisal report that satisfies the agreed upon scope of work with 1021098645 Saskatchewan Ltd. The purpose of this assignment is to provide the As Stabilized current market value of the subject property, which at the time of inspection represents the improved property as of the effective date and is leased up at market rental rates and operating costs for the property located at 1101, 1121 109 St, North Battleford, SK (herein after referred to as the subject property).
elements.description: Scope of work and assignment purpose narrative

elements.currentValue: The subject property, located at 1101, 1121 109 St, North Battleford, SK, is a multi-family, walkup property with improvements located in North Battleford. The improvements are comprised of 2 total buildings, and consist of 19,204 square feet of rentable area (NRA) as of the valuation date. The property, reportedly built in 1970 (1970 weighted) is approximately 100.0% occupied and features 16 units in a 2-story, garden style format.
elements.description: Executive summary property description with key metrics
Effective date of valuation in table

elements.currentValue: The use of a hypothetical condition(s) may have impacted the results of the assignment. The As Stabilized value has been developed in part based on the hypothetical condition that all units could achieve current market rent levels and has achieved stabilized occupancy as of the effective date of the appraisal. Under this premise, no additional rental income concessions (such as free rent, move-in specials, or the like) are provided. In reality, as of the effective date, the property's existing lease terms reflect contract rents that are deemed to be below-market rents. For the purposes of this analysis, it is assumed that lease-up to market rent levels has occurred under typical market conditions, without undue delay or concessions exceeding market norms. If this assumption proves incorrect, such as market rents are not achievable the value conclusion may be materially impacted.
No Extraordinary Assumptions were made for this assignment.
No Extraordinary Limiting Conditions were made for this assignment.

pages.title: Introduction & Executive Summary (Hypothetical Conditions and Assumptions)

elements.currentValue: The use of a hypothetical condition(s) may have impacted the results of the assignment. The As Stabilized value has been developed based on the hypothetical condition that the subject property is fully leased at prevailing market rents and has achieved stabilized occupancy as of the effective date of the appraisal. Under this premise, no deductions are made for holding costs, rent loss, or lease-up expenses. In addition it is a hypothetical condition that all units could achieve current market rent levels and stabilized occupancy as of the effective date. In reality, as of the effective date, the property's existing lease terms reflect contract rents that are deemed to be below-market rents. For purposes of this analysis, it is assumed that lease-up to market rent levels has occurred under typical market conditions, without undue delay or concessions exceeding market norms. If this assumption proves incorrect, such as market rents are not achievable the value conclusion may be materially impacted.
No Extraordinary Assumptions were made for this assignment.
No Extraordinary Limiting Conditions were made for this assignment.
